zh.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johns是Johns和Sarah Perry的女兒，於1781年9月21日出生在美國新澤西州薩萊姆。她於1804年9月1日與Parker Sullivan在美國新澤西州薩萊姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她在美國俄亥俄州哈帕易恩哈里森 堂史普住了大約10年。她去世於1864年4月2日在美國俄亥俄州哈帕易恩厄巴納 堂史普厄巴納享年82歲，埋葬在美國俄亥俄州哈帕易恩哈里森 堂史普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johns是Johns和Sarah Perry的女儿，于1781年9月21日出生在美国新泽西州薩萊姆。她于1804年9月1日与Parker Sullivan在美国新泽西州薩萊姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她在美国俄亥俄州哈帕易恩哈里森 堂史普住了大约10年。她去世于1864年4月2日在美国俄亥俄州哈帕易恩厄巴納 堂史普厄巴納享年82岁，埋葬在美国俄亥俄州哈帕易恩哈里森 堂史普。

nameMeanings.name: Johns
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of John with genitival or excrescent -s, or a variant of Jones . It is common in Cornwall and Devon, south Wales, the West Midlands and Lancashire. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, such as Albanian Gjonaj or any other Albanian patronymic from the personal name Gjon ‘John’ and its variants or derivatives; see also examples at Johnson .
German: patronymic from   John   or a shortened form of   Johannes  .
Americanized form of Swiss German   Schantz  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Johnes
meanings: English and German: variant of   Johns   or   Jones  . This form of the surname is also found in Finland.

nameMeanings.name: Martha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, of Aramaic rather than Hebrew origin, meaning ‘lady’. It was borne by the sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (John 11:1). According to Luke 10:38, when Jesus visited the house of Mary and Martha, Mary sat at his feet, listening to him, while Martha ‘was cumbered about much serving’, so that she complained to Jesus, ‘Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone?’ For this reason, the name Martha has always been associated with hard domestic work, as opposed to the contemplative life.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: New Jersey Plan
historicEvents.description: "Also referred to as the Small State Plan, the New Jersey Plan was an important piece of legislation that William Paterson presented during the Constitutional Convention. The plan was created because states with smaller populations were concerned about their representation in the United States government. The New Jersey plan proposed, among other things, that each state would have one equal vote. This was in contrast to the Virginia Plan, which suggested that appointment for Congress should be proportional to state population. The Connecticut Compromise merged the two plans, allowing for two ""houses"" of congress: one with proportional representation, and the other with equal power from each state (as the New Jersey Plan had suggested)."
Ohio was the first state admitted to the Union from the Northwest Territory.

Alexander Hamilton and Aaron Burr had been political enemies with intense personal differences for quite some time. Burr accused Hamilton of publicly disparaging his character during the elections of 1800 and 1804. On the morning of July 11, the two politicians went to Weehawken, New Jersey to resolve the disputes with an official duel. Both men were armed with a pistol. Hamilton missed, but Burr's shot fatally wounded Hamilton, who would die by the following day. The duel custom had been outlawed in New York by 1804, resulting in Burr fleeing the state due to an arrest warrant. He would later be accused of treason, but ultimately be acquitted.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Library of Congress - Today In History: July 11 (A Tragic Duel)

historicEvents.description: The Camden & Amboy railroad was completed in 1833. It made transportation between New York City and Philadelphia possible within a 7-hour time-frame. This advancement led to dozens of other companies opening railroad lines during the 1800's.
historicEvents.urlTitle: New Jersey in the 19th Century : Transportation

historicEvents.title: Kirtland Temple Dedicated
historicEvents.description: On March 27, 1836, the Kirtland Temple was dedicated.

A battalion of volunteers from New Jersey assisted during the Mexican-American War. They were active from 1847 to 1848 and divided among four companies. The battalion assisted during the Battle of Contreras and the Battle of Churubusco.
historicEvents.urlTitle: History of New Jersey (War and Slavery)

historicEvents.title: Ohio supports the Union side of the Civil War
historicEvents.description: Although divided as a state on the subject of slavery, Ohio participated in the Civil War on the Union's side, providing over 300,000 troops. Ohio provided the 3rd largest number of troops by any Union state.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Ohio supports the Union side of the Civil War

en.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johns was born on 21 September 1781, in Salem, New Jersey, United States as the daughter of Johns and Sarah Perry. She married Parker Sullivan on 1 September 1804, in Salem, New Jersey, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She lived in Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, United States for about 10 years. She died on 2 April 1864, in Urbana, Urbana Township, Champaign, Ohio, United States, at the age of 82, and was buried in Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johnsは1781年9月21日にアメリカ合衆国ニュージャージー州サレムでJohnsとSarah Perryの娘として生まれた。1804年9月1日にアメリカ合衆国ニュージャージー州サレムでParker Sullivanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女はアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州チャンペイグンハーリソン トウンシュイップに約10年間住んでいた。彼女は1864年4月2日にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州チャンペイグンウルバナ トウンシュイップウルバナで82歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国オハイオ州チャンペイグンハーリソン トウンシュイップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johns(은)는 1781년 9월 21일 미국 뉴저지 주 살렘에서 Johns(와)과 Sarah Perry의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1804년 9월 1일 미국 뉴저지 주 살렘에서 Parker Sullivan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 오하이오 주 챔파익 해리슨 타운시프에서 약 10년 동안 살았다. 그녀는 1864년 4월 2일 미국 오하이오 주 챔파익 어바나 타운시프 어바나에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 오하이오 주 챔파익 해리슨 타운시프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johns nasceu em 21 de setembro de 1781, em Salem, Nova Jérsei, Estados Unidos da América filha de Johns e Sarah Perry. Ela casou-se com Parker Sullivan em 1 de setembro de 1804, em Salem, Nova Jérsei, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela viveu em Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América por quase 10 anos. Ela faleceu em 2 de abril de 1864, em Urbana, Urbana Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América, com 82 anos, e foi sepultada em Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johns родилась 21 сентября 1781 г. в местности сеилем, Нью-Джерси, Соединенные штаты. Её родителей звали Johns и Sarah Perry. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Parker Sullivan 1 сентября 1804 г. в местности сеилем, Нью-Джерси, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она жила в местности харрисон тоуншип, чампеигн, Огайо, Соединенные штаты около 10 лет. Она умерла 2 апреля 1864 г. в местности урбана, урбана тоуншип, чампеигн, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности харрисон тоуншип, чампеигн, Огайо, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Martha Mary Johns nació el 21 de septiembre de 1781, en Salem, Nueva Jersey, Estados Unidos como hija de Johns y Sarah Perry. Se casó con Parker Sullivan el 1 de septiembre de 1804, en Salem, Nueva Jersey, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos durante unos 10 años. Murió el 2 de abril de 1864, en Urbana, Urbana Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultada en Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos.
